title: RED tests + TaintSinkType extension scaffold + perf baseline
implemented: Added 4 ADDITIVE TaintSinkType variants (HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize) at crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs after FileWrite. Existing 6 variants (SqlQuery, CodeEval, CodeExec, CodeCompile, ShellExec, FileWrite) preserved verbatim — additive only per validator_mandate taintsinktype_extension_additive_only.

Updated test_taint_sink_type_variants at crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs:202: variant array extended from 6 to 10, assert_eq! variants.len() == 10, doc-comment block extended with 4 new bullets (XSS sink, path-traversal sink, SSRF sink, deser sink). test_taint_source_type_variants left untouched (variants.len() == 6) — TaintSourceType is NOT extended.

Authored 166 fixtures across 83 (lang, vuln_type) pairs from plan §4.2 X-marks matrix at crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/<lang>/{positive,string_literal_fp}.<ext>. Note: actual matrix yields 83 pairs (16 SqlInjection + 8 Xss + 17 CommandInjection + 17 PathTraversal + 7 Ssrf + 12 Deserialization + matrix re-counted from plan.md), not the 33 cited in the dispatch contract or 66 cited in plan §4.2 narrative — the matrix at plan §4.2 is authoritative and yields 83 X-marks when expanding TS/JS into 2 separate columns.

Authored 166 RED tests at crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_migration_v1_red.rs (one positive + one string-literal regression-guard per pair). Each test invokes the binary via assert_cmd cargo_bin!, runs `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang <L> --format json --quiet`, and asserts ≥1 finding (positive) or ZERO findings (FP regression-guard).

Authored composite RED test at crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_migration_v1_composite_red.rs + composite Go fixture at crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/composite/multi_pattern.go — single file with all 6 source-pattern strings + all 6 sink-pattern strings inside string literals/comments. Asserts ZERO findings (closes-#24 root pattern at file scale).

Created continuum/autonomous/vuln-migration-v1-plan/perf-fixture/ with 20 representative Go files: 5 large (~380 LOC, 12-15 fn each, HTTP+db+JSON), 5 medium (~56 LOC, 4-6 fn), 10 small (~20 LOC, 2 fn). Real Go idioms — NOT lorem.

Captured M1 perf baseline: 3 directory-level runs averaged 36.67ms; per-file timings 30-34ms (binary startup dominated, p99 = 34ms). Recorded to reports/M1-perf-baseline.json for the M3 two-axis perf gate (avg < 2× AND p99-file < 5×).

checks.cargo_check_workspace: PASS — clean build, no non-exhaustive match warnings (validator_mandate taintsinktype_extension_additive_only — codebase uses matches!() filters and struct-literal construction; zero exhaustive match-on-TaintSinkType sites needed extension)
checks.cargo_test_p_tldr_core_release_lib: PASS — 4674 passed, 0 failed, 340 ignored. test_taint_sink_type_variants GREEN at variants.len() == 10
checks.cargo_test_p_tldr_cli_release_test_vuln_: RED-AS-EXPECTED — 108 passed, 58 failed (37 string-literal FP fails on the fall-through langs + 21 positive fails where source/sink span lines or pattern not substring-matched). The 37 FP fails ARE the closes-#24 root pattern; M3 flips them GREEN.
RED-AS-EXPECTED — 1 fail (substring scanner emits ≥1 finding from string-literal patterns); M3 flips this GREEN.
checks.preexisting_orthogonal_failures_unchange: 5 unrelated failures observed in tldr-core integration tests (tree_handles_empty_directory, test_surface_unsupported_language_errors, test_realistic_python_project_structure, test_git_log_empty_repo, test_git_log_numstat_empty_repo). These are pre-existing, NOT regressions from M1 — none touch taint/vuln modules.

loc_delta_actual.delta_explanation: Spec estimated ~280 LOC for the source/test code only (taint.rs +4 variants, taint_tests.rs +4 entries, ~3 match-site arms). Actual source/test source-code delta is 17 LOC. Remaining ~6171 LOC is fixtures + RED test bodies + perf-fixture + reports — all artifacts mandated by validator (m1_perf_fixture_directory_required, string_literal_fp_regression_mandatory, m1_test_enumeration_required, etc.). The 280 LOC figure in the contract was a code-only estimate; total artifact LOC was always going to be much higher and is sized appropriately to the matrix.

discoveries: Matrix counting: plan §4.2 contains 16+8+17+17+7+12 = 77 X-marks treating TS/JS as one column. Expanding TS/JS into separate `typescript` + `javascript` lang_arg yields 83 pairs (each marked TS/JS row pair becomes 2 fixtures). Generated 83 pairs × 2 fixtures = 166 files. Contract estimate of 33 pairs and plan estimate of ~66 fixtures both undercounted.

Match-site fallout: zero. Codebase consistently uses `matches!(s.sink_type, TaintSinkType::ShellExec)` filters (27 sites in tests) and `sink_type: TaintSinkType::X` struct-literal construction (60+ sites in taint.rs); zero exhaustive match arms over TaintSinkType. Validator_mandate `taintsinktype_extension_additive_only` satisfied trivially. Reports/M1-match-site-audit.json documents the audit.

Per-file perf timings dominated by binary startup (~30ms baseline). Directory-level runs (37ms for 20 files) amortize startup and provide the meaningful avg-axis baseline. P99-file = 34ms (close to baseline) means the M3 p99-file gate (< 170ms) has plenty of headroom.

Obsolete-test count is 32 (not 36 or 26). Reports/M1-test-enumeration.json lists each by file:line with decision DELETE/PRESERVE and category; 1 entry (test_string_interpolation_detection) is DECISION-PENDING-M4 awaiting confirmation that canonical engine covers f-string flow.

compliance.explicit_staging_only: OK — only listed files staged via `git add <files>`
compliance.validator_mandate_obsolete_test_count_au: OK — reports/M1-test-enumeration.json committed with 32 obsolete tests by file:line + 13 preserved entries
compliance.validator_mandate_red_first_harness_requ: OK — RED state captured in reports/M1-red-capture.{txt,json}
compliance.validator_mandate_string_literal_fp_regr: OK — 83 string-literal regression-guard fixtures + 1 composite committed (M3 will flip to GREEN)
compliance.validator_mandate_no_taintinfo_taintflow: OK — TaintInfo / TaintFlow API at taint.rs:217-250 untouched. New variants integrate via the existing TaintSink.sink_type field

stop_thresholds.cargo_test_workspace_passes_including_te: PASS for tldr-core lib (4674/4674); RED-as-expected for vuln_migration_v1_red (gating for M2/M3)
stop_thresholds.RED_capture_string_literal_regression_FA: PASS — 37/83 FP tests fail at HEAD on the fall-through langs (FP class active); composite FAILS
stop_thresholds.RED_capture_positive_PASS_at_HEAD: PARTIAL — 62/83 positives PASS at HEAD; 21 fail (mostly substring scanner can't link source+sink across lines, or specific patterns not substring-matched). All 21 will become GREEN by end of M2/M3 once AST detection is wired and dispatch flips to canonical.

next_milestone: M2 — extend AST sink banks for HtmlOutput / FileOpen / HttpRequest / Deserialize across 16 LanguagePatterns. Estimated ~218 new AstSinkPattern entries.
